items.title: Global software stocks extend losses amid fears over AI-led disruption
items.description: Wall Street's fears around artificial intelligence-driven disruption affecting software companies made their way to Asia and Europe.

items.title: Stock Market Today, Feb. 3: Nasdaq Tumbles 1.4% as AI Fears Grow
items.description: Today, Feb. 3, 2026, Walmart’s $1 trillion milestone stands out as rising bond yields and tech weakness drag major U.S. indexes lower.

items.title: Threat of New AI Tools Wipes $300 Billion Off Software and Data Stocks
items.description: From Legalzoom.com and Expedia to Ares and Apollo, shares of companies that sell or invest in software fell sharply on Tuesday.

items.title: Heard on the Street Recap: Code Red
items.description: Anthropic's launch of new AI productivity tools spooked investors. The worries hit a wide swath of software and data service companies. Among the worst-hit companies on the Nasdaq were Thomson Reuters and Intuit, which shed 16% and 11%, respectively.

items.title: Software Stocks Have Gotten Crushed. This Chart Says It’s Not Time to Buy Yet.
items.description: “Old tech” software names like  Adobe  and  Intuit  are both off more than 40% from their annual peaks.  The weakness is especially glaring on the IGV ETF’s ratio chart versus the  While IGV trades 28% below its most recent 52-week high, the semiconductor ETF is down just 5% from its own annual peak.
